info.title: Helius API Catalog
info.summary: Machine-readable discovery document for every Helius API surface on Solana.
info.description:
This document is a discovery catalog — not an operational spec. It exists so
AI agents, codegen tools, and SDK generators can locate the canonical
OpenAPI document for every Helius API in one request instead of crawling
the docs site.

Start with the `x-apis` array: each entry links a Helius API surface to its
canonical OpenAPI spec (`specUrl`) and human-readable docs (`docsUrl`).
Follow `externalDocs` for the full developer documentation site.

## A note on JSON-RPC APIs

Several Helius APIs (Solana RPC, DAS, Sender, Mint, Priority Fee,
ZK Compression) follow the JSON-RPC 2.0 convention where every method POSTs
to `/` with a `method` field in the request body. For each such API the
repository ships both per-method fragment YAMLs (used to render the
Mintlify reference pages) and an auto-generated `_combined.yaml` that
merges every method into a single OpenAPI document. In the combined
documents, colliding paths are disambiguated with a URL-fragment suffix
(e.g. `/#getAccountInfo`) and each operation carries an `x-actual-path`
extension that records the real request path (`/`). The fragment syntax is
schema-only — real calls still go to the `url` in `servers`.

All `specUrl` values below point to raw GitHub files on the `main` branch
of `helius-labs/docs`. They are stable URLs suitable for downstream tools
to pin or fetch.

servers.description: Solana mainnet RPC endpoint (Solana RPC, DAS, Priority Fee, ZK Compression)
Helius REST APIs (Wallet API, Enhanced API, Webhooks)
Helius Admin API — project usage and billing

get.summary: Returns all NFT related events associated with the given address.
get.description:
Retrieve comprehensive NFT events data for any Solana address. Filter by event types, 
transaction sources, and time ranges to get precise NFT activity including mints, 
sales, listings, and transfers.

get.summary: Returns an enhanced transaction history for a given address
get.description:
Access comprehensive transaction history for any Solana address with human-readable 
decoded data. Filter by transaction types, sources, and time ranges to get detailed
insights into wallet activity with context and decoded instructions.

200.description: Returns an array of enhanced transactions.

get.summary: Returns raw transaction history for a given address
get.description:
Retrieve complete raw transaction data for any Solana address with full
transaction details in their original format. Access historical transaction 
data with precise time filtering options.

200.description: Returns an array of raw transactions.

post.summary: Returns an array of enhanced, human-readable versions of the given transactions.
post.description:
Convert raw Solana transactions into enhanced, human-readable formats with
decoded instruction data and contextual information. Process multiple transactions
in a single request for efficient data analysis and display.

200.description: Returns an array of enhanced transactions.

post.summary: Returns an array of raw (directly from RPC) transactions.

200.description: Returns an array of raw transactions.

post.summary: Returns token metadata (whether NFT or Fungible) for a given token address.

get.summary: Returns the Solana Naming Service name for a given address.

200.description: Returns the Solana Naming Service name if exists. Otherwise returns null.

get.summary: Returns the native balance and token balances for a given address.

200.description: Returns the native balance and the token balances for a given account.

get.summary: Returns the NFTs held for a given address.

200.description: Returns the current NFTs that this address owns.

post.summary: Returns a list of mint accounts for a given NFT collection.
Returns an array of raw transactions for the given accounts.
Returns all metadata associated with an account, including data from the old token list.

get.description:
Retrieve a complete list of all configured webhooks for your API key with detailed
configuration information including trigger conditions, delivery endpoints, and status.

200.description: Webhooks for the given API key.

post.description:
Create a new webhook for real-time Solana blockchain notifications. Configure triggers 
based on addresses, transaction types, or specific on-chain events. Customize delivery 
options and notification formatting for seamless integration with your application.

get.description:
Retrieve detailed information about a specific webhook configuration by its unique ID.
Access complete webhook settings including trigger conditions, delivery endpoints, and status metrics.

200.description: Returns details about the given webhook ID.

put.description:
Update an existing webhook configuration with new trigger conditions, delivery endpoints,
or notification formatting. Modify your real-time blockchain notification settings to adapt
to changing application requirements without service interruption.

delete.description:
Permanently remove a webhook configuration from your account. Immediately stop
receiving notifications for the specified webhook trigger conditions and free up
resources for other webhook configurations.

patch.summary: Toggle a webhook on or off.
patch.description:
Enable or disable a webhook without deleting it. Use this to re-enable a webhook that
was automatically disabled due to a high endpoint failure rate, or to temporarily pause
deliveries. After re-enabling, the webhook enters a 24-hour grace period during which it
will not be automatically disabled again.

get.description:
Retrieve identity information for a known wallet (exchanges, protocols, etc.). Accepts
either a Solana wallet address or an SNS (`.sol`) / ANS custom TLD domain name
(e.g. `toly.sol`, `miester.bonk`). Domain resolution is mainnet-only.

The response is the standard identity object for the **resolved address**. No domain
marker (`inputDomain`) is attached on this endpoint — use `POST /v1/wallet/batch-identity`
if you need to correlate inputs with outputs.

Returns `404` if the wallet has no identity entry, or if a domain input cannot be resolved.
Returns `400` if the input is neither a valid address nor a valid domain name, or if a
domain input is sent on a non-mainnet deployment.

404.description: No identity information available, or domain could not be resolved

post.description:
Retrieve identity information for multiple entries in a single request. Each entry may be
either a Solana wallet address or an SNS (`.sol`) / ANS custom TLD domain name. Domain
resolution is mainnet-only.

Unresolvable domain entries do not fail the batch — they come back as
`{ "address": null, "type": "unknown", "inputDomain": "...", "unresolved": true }`
in request order. Resolved domain entries carry an `inputDomain` field so callers can
correlate responses with the original input.

addresses.description: Array of Solana wallet addresses and/or SNS/ANS domain names to look up (up to 100 per request).

200.description: Identity information for requested entries, in request order

get.description:
Retrieve token and NFT balances for a wallet. **Pagination is manual** - the API returns up to 100 tokens per request.

Results are sorted by USD value in descending order. Tokens with pricing data appear first, followed by tokens without prices.

**Pagination:** Use the `page` parameter to fetch additional pages. The response includes `pagination.hasMore`
to indicate if more results are available. Each request makes a single API call and costs 100 credits.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of tokens per page

parameters.name: showNfts
parameters.description: Include NFTs in results (max 100, first page only)

get.description:
Retrieve transaction history for a wallet using the Enhanced Transactions API.
Returns human-readable, parsed transactions with balance changes for each transaction.
**Pagination is manual** - the API returns up to 100 transactions per request.

Returns transactions in reverse chronological order (newest first).

**Associated Token Accounts (ATAs):** The `tokenAccounts` parameter controls whether transactions
involving token accounts owned by the wallet are included:
- `balanceChanged` (recommended): Includes transactions that changed token account balances, filtering spam
- `none`: Only direct wallet interactions
- `all`: All token account transactions including spam

**Pagination:** Use the `before` parameter with `pagination.nextCursor` to fetch the next page.
The response includes `pagination.hasMore` to indicate if more results are available.
Each request makes a single API call and costs 100 credits.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of transactions per request

parameters.name: before
parameters.description: Fetch transactions before this signature (use `pagination.nextCursor` from previous response)

parameters.name: after
parameters.description: Fetch transactions after this signature (for ascending order pagination)

parameters.name: type
parameters.description:
Filter by transaction type. Available types: SWAP, TRANSFER, NFT_SALE, NFT_BID, NFT_LISTING,
NFT_MINT, NFT_CANCEL_LISTING, TOKEN_MINT, BURN, COMPRESSED_NFT_MINT, COMPRESSED_NFT_TRANSFER,
COMPRESSED_NFT_BURN, CREATE_STORE, WHITELIST_CREATOR, ADD_TO_WHITELIST, REMOVE_FROM_WHITELIST,
AUCTION_MANAGER_CLAIM_BID, EMPTY_PAYMENT_ACCOUNT, UPDATE_PRIMARY_SALE_METADATA, ADD_TOKEN_TO_VAULT,
ACTIVATE_VAULT, INIT_VAULT, INIT_BANK, INIT_STAKE, MERGE_STAKE, SPLIT_STAKE, CREATE_AUCTION_MANAGER,
START_AUCTION, CREATE_AUCTION_MANAGER_V2, UPDATE_EXTERNAL_PRICE_ACCOUNT, EXECUTE_TRANSACTION

parameters.name: tokenAccounts
parameters.description:
Filter transactions involving token accounts owned by the wallet.
- `balanceChanged` (recommended): Includes transactions that changed token balances, filters spam
- `none`: Only transactions directly referencing the wallet
- `all`: All transactions including token accounts (may include spam)

get.description:
Retrieve all token transfer activity for a wallet including sender/recipient information.
Returns transfers in reverse chronological order (newest first).

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of transfers to return

get.description:
Discover the original funding source of a wallet by analyzing its first incoming SOL transfer.
Useful for identifying if a wallet was funded by an exchange, another wallet, etc.

post.description: Submit transactions via Helius Sender for ultra-low latency transaction submission with dual routing to both Solana validators and Jito infrastructure.

parameters.name: api-key
parameters.description: Standard users do not require an API key. Only users with custom TPS limits need to provide an API key.

parameters.name: swqos_only
parameters.description: When true, routes exclusively through SWQOS infrastructure, but has a lower minimal tip requirement.

id.description: A unique identifier for the request.

method.description: The name of the RPC method to invoke.

params.description: Parameters for sending a transaction via Sender.

encoding.description: Data encoding format used for the Solana transaction payload.

skipPreflight.description: When true, bypasses Solana's preflight transaction validation for faster submission. Must be set to true for Sender.

maxRetries.description: Maximum number of automatic retry attempts. Should be set to 0.

200.description: Transaction submitted successfully. Since we're skipping preflight checks, a 200 response does not guarantee that the transaction is valid or that it will land onchain.

result.description: The transaction signature for tracking confirmation status.

400.description: Bad Request - Missing tip, priority fee, or invalid submission parameters.

429.description: Too Many Requests - Rate limit exceeded.

500.description: Internal Server Error - An error occurred on the server.

503.description: Service Unavailable - The service is temporarily unavailable.

504.description: Gateway Timeout - The request timed out.

get.description: Ping request to Helius Sender to maintain a warm connection

503.description: Service Unavailable - The service is temporarily unavailable.

post.description:
Calculate optimal priority fee recommendations for Solana transactions based on real-time 
network conditions. This advanced API analyzes historical fee data and current congestion 
levels to provide precise fee estimates for different priority levels.

200.description: Successfully retrieved Solana priority fee estimates

401.description: Unauthorized — missing or invalid API key.

429.description: Too Many Requests — rate limit exceeded.

500.description: Internal server error. Retryable with exponential backoff.

get.description:
Retrieve credit usage, subscription details, and per-service request counts for a project within the current billing cycle.

The API key used for authentication must belong to the project specified in the path. Requests where the API key's project does not match the `id` parameter will return a `400` error.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The project ID to retrieve usage for. Must match the project associated with the API key.

400.description: Bad Request — the project ID in the path does not match the project associated with the API key.

401.description: Unauthorized — API key is missing, malformed, or not found.

403.description: Forbidden — the Admin API is not enabled for this project.

example.message: Admin API not enabled for this project

429.description: Too Many Requests — rate limit of 5 requests per second exceeded.

500.description: Internal Server Error — a server-side error occurred (e.g., missing billing data).

x-apis.name: Solana RPC (HTTP)
x-apis.description: JSON-RPC 2.0 methods for Solana mainnet and devnet, plus Helius extensions such as `getTransactionsForAddress` with server-side filtering and token account helpers (`getTokenAccountsByOwnerV2`, `getProgramAccountsV2`).

x-apis.name: Digital Asset Standard (DAS) API
x-apis.description: Unified NFT and token queries for Solana — regular NFTs, compressed NFTs, and fungible tokens — indexed for fast lookups by owner, creator, authority, collection, or search.

x-apis.name: Helius Sender
x-apis.description: Ultra-low-latency transaction submission with dual routing to validators and Jito infrastructure. Regional endpoints for Salt Lake City, Newark, London, Frankfurt, Amsterdam, Singapore, and Tokyo.

x-apis.name: Wallet API
x-apis.description: High-performance REST API for Solana wallet data — identity, balances, transaction history, transfers, and funding lineage. Returns pre-indexed results suitable for wallet and portfolio UIs.

x-apis.name: Priority Fee API
x-apis.description: Real-time priority fee recommendations across multiple priority levels, derived from recent network activity. Accepts either account keys or a serialized transaction.

x-apis.name: ZK Compression Indexer
x-apis.description: Indexer API for Solana state compression — compressed accounts, compressed token balances, Merkle proofs, and validity proofs. Enables up to 1000x lower storage costs relative to regular accounts.

x-apis.name: Admin API
x-apis.description: Programmatic access to Helius project usage and billing data — credits consumed, credits remaining, prepaid balances, and subscription details for the current billing cycle.

x-apis.name: Enhanced API
x-apis.description: Decoded, human-readable transaction history, NFT events, and address activity on Solana. Returns typed, parsed data instead of raw instruction bytes.

x-apis.name: Helius Webhooks
x-apis.description: Real-time HTTP notifications for Solana on-chain events — configure account, transaction, or NFT event subscriptions and receive parsed payloads via HTTPS POST.

authHeader.description: Optional authorization header value included in webhook deliveries for verifying the sender.

active.description: Whether the webhook is actively receiving deliveries. Defaults to true. Webhooks may be automatically disabled if the endpoint has a high failure rate.
Set to true to enable the webhook or false to disable it.

domainNames.description: All on-chain domain names owned by this address, including SNS (.sol) and ANS custom TLDs (.bonk, .wen, etc.). The favorite .sol domain is first if set; remaining domains are sorted alphabetically. Omitted if the wallet owns no domains.

WalletApi_BatchResolvedIdentity.description:
A resolved entry in a batch identity response. Extends `Identity` with an optional
`inputDomain` field that is present when the original batch input for this entry was a
domain name. Returned only from `POST /v1/wallet/batch-identity`.

inputDomain.description: The domain name the caller queried. Present only when a domain (not an address) was provided as input for this batch entry.

WalletApi_UnresolvedDomainEntry.description:
A batch entry returned when a domain name could not be resolved. Only appears as an
element of the array returned by `POST /v1/wallet/batch-identity` — the single-endpoint
returns `404` instead of this shape.

type.description: Always the literal string `"unknown"` for unresolved entries.

inputDomain.description: The domain name the caller queried that could not be resolved.

WalletApi_BatchIdentityEntry.description:
One element of the array returned by `POST /v1/wallet/batch-identity`. Resolved entries
match `BatchResolvedIdentity` (possibly with `inputDomain`); unresolvable domain entries
match `UnresolvedDomainEntry`. Distinguish at runtime by checking `unresolved === true`
or `address === null`.

tokenProgram.description: Token program type (spl-token for legacy, token-2022 for new standard)

compressed.description: Whether this is a compressed NFT

balances.description:
Array of token balances for the current page, including native SOL.
When showNative=true, SOL appears as the first element with mint address So11111111111111111111111111111111111111112.
Other tokens are sorted by USD value (descending).

nfts.description: Array of NFT holdings (only included if showNfts=true, max 100, first page only)

totalUsdValue.description: Total USD value of balances on this page (not total portfolio value)

pagination.description: Pagination metadata. Users must manually request additional pages using the page parameter.

hasMore.description: True if more results are available. Increment the page parameter to fetch the next page.

amount.description: Change amount (positive for increase, negative for decrease)

feePayer.description: Address that paid the transaction fee

balanceChanges.description: All balance changes in this transaction

direction.description: Transfer direction relative to the wallet

counterparty.description: The other party in the transfer (sender if 'in', recipient if 'out')

amountRaw.description: Raw transfer amount in smallest unit (lamports for SOL, raw token amount for SPL tokens)

funder.description: Address that originally funded this wallet

funderName.description: Name of the funder if it's a known entity

amountRaw.description: Raw funding amount in smallest unit (lamports for SOL)

signature.description: Transaction signature of the funding transfer

date.description: Human-readable UTC date in ISO 8601 format

explorerUrl.description: Solana Explorer URL for the transaction

nextCursor.description: Cursor to fetch the next page of results

details.example: 'invalid-address' is not a valid Solana address

jsonrpc.description: JSON-RPC version identifier for the Solana RPC API request, must be "2.0"

transaction.description:
Base58 or Base64 encoded Solana transaction for which to estimate optimal priority fees.
The API will analyze the specific instructions and accounts in this transaction to provide
accurate fee estimates based on current network conditions and computational complexity.

accountKeys.description: Array of Solana account public keys to estimate priority fees for (alternative to providing a transaction)

items.description: Base58 encoded public key of a Solana account

PriorityFeeApi_PriorityFeeOptions.description: Advanced options for customizing Solana priority fee estimation based on network conditions

transactionEncoding.description: Encoding format of the provided Solana transaction

priorityLevel.description: Specific priority level to estimate fees for based on current Solana network congestion

includeAllPriorityFeeLevels.description: When true, returns comprehensive estimates for all priority levels from minimum to maximum

lookbackSlots.description: Number of recent Solana blockchain slots to analyze for accurate fee estimation (1-150)

includeVote.description: When true, includes vote transactions in the fee calculation for more comprehensive analysis

recommended.description: When true, returns the recommended optimal fee based on current Solana network conditions

evaluateEmptySlotAsZero.description: When true, slots with no transactions are counted as zero-fee slots in the calculation

result.description: Result object containing comprehensive Solana priority fee estimates

priorityFeeEstimate.description:
Estimated optimal priority fee in microlamports for the requested Solana transaction,
calculated based on recent blockchain activity and computational requirements.

priorityFeeLevels.description:
Detailed fee estimates for different priority levels on the Solana blockchain (in microlamports),
allowing developers to choose the optimal balance between cost and confirmation speed.

min.description:
Minimum viable fee for Solana transactions - suitable for non-urgent transactions during low congestion periods
(may result in transaction failure during network congestion)

low.description:
Low priority fee for Solana transactions - economical option for transactions that aren't time-sensitive
(longer confirmation time, lower cost)

medium.description:
Medium priority fee for Solana transactions - balanced option for most standard transactions
(reasonable confirmation time with moderate cost)

high.description:
High priority fee for Solana transactions - prioritized processing for time-sensitive operations
like DeFi trades or NFT mints (faster confirmation, higher cost)

veryHigh.description:
Very high priority fee for Solana transactions - premium option for critical transactions requiring
near-immediate confirmation (very fast confirmation, premium cost)

unsafeMax.description:
Maximum observed fee on Solana - the highest fee seen in the analysis window, representing
an upper bound during extreme congestion (fastest possible confirmation, but potentially unnecessarily high)

creditsRemaining.description: Number of credits remaining in the current billing cycle. Calculated as `creditsLimit - regularCreditsUsed`, floored at 0.

creditsUsed.description: Total credits consumed in the current billing cycle, including both regular and prepaid credits.

prepaidCreditsUsed.description: Number of prepaid credits consumed in the current billing cycle.

subscriptionDetails.description: Details about the project's subscription plan and current billing cycle.

billingCycle.description: Start and end dates of the current billing cycle.

creditsLimit.description: Total credit allowance for the current billing cycle based on the plan.

plan.description: The name of the subscription plan.

usage.description: Request counts broken down by service type for the current billing cycle.

api.description: Number of Enhanced API requests (e.g., parsed transactions, token metadata).

das.description: Number of DAS (Digital Asset Standard) API requests.

rpc.description: Number of standard Solana RPC requests.

stream.description: Number of LaserStream data streaming requests.

EnhancedApi_limitParam.name: limit
EnhancedApi_limitParam.description: Maximum number of items to return.

EnhancedApi_beforeParam.name: before
EnhancedApi_beforeParam.description: Start searching backwards from this transaction signature.

EnhancedApi_beforeSigParam.name: before-signature
EnhancedApi_beforeSigParam.description: Start searching backwards from this transaction signature.

EnhancedApi_afterSigParam.name: after-signature
EnhancedApi_afterSigParam.description: Start searching forwards from this transaction signature.

EnhancedApi_sortOrderParam.name: sort-order
EnhancedApi_sortOrderParam.description: The order to sort the results in.

EnhancedApi_tokenAccountsParam.name: token-accounts
EnhancedApi_tokenAccountsParam.description: Filter transactions for related token accounts. Controls whether to include transactions involving token accounts owned by the address.

EnhancedApi_gtSlotParam.name: gt-slot
EnhancedApi_gtSlotParam.description: Only return transactions with a slot greater than this value.

EnhancedApi_gteSlotParam.name: gte-slot
EnhancedApi_gteSlotParam.description: Only return transactions with a slot greater than or equal to this value.

EnhancedApi_ltSlotParam.name: lt-slot
EnhancedApi_ltSlotParam.description: Only return transactions with a slot less than this value.

EnhancedApi_lteSlotParam.name: lte-slot
EnhancedApi_lteSlotParam.description: Only return transactions with a slot less than or equal to this value.

EnhancedApi_gtTimeParam.name: gt-time
EnhancedApi_gtTimeParam.description: Only return transactions with a block time greater than this value.

EnhancedApi_gteTimeParam.name: gte-time
EnhancedApi_gteTimeParam.description: Only return transactions with a block time greater than or equal to this value.

EnhancedApi_ltTimeParam.name: lt-time
EnhancedApi_ltTimeParam.description: Only return transactions with a block time less than this value.

EnhancedApi_lteTimeParam.name: lte-time
EnhancedApi_lteTimeParam.description: Only return transactions with a block time less than or equal to this value.

EnhancedApi_commitmentParam.name: commitment
EnhancedApi_commitmentParam.description: How finalized a block must be to be included in the search. If not provided, will default to "finalized" commitment. Note that "processed" level commitment is not supported.

EnhancedApi_webhookIDParam.name: webhookID
EnhancedApi_webhookIDParam.description: The webhook ID — a UUIDv4 that uniquely identifies the webhook.

EnhancedApi_pageNumberParam.name: pageNumber
EnhancedApi_pageNumberParam.description: The page number used for pagination.

EnhancedApi_transactionIdParam.name: transaction-id
EnhancedApi_transactionIdParam.description: The signature of the transaction you wish to query for.

EnhancedApi_transactionSourceParam.name: source
EnhancedApi_transactionSourceParam.description: The TransactionSource to filter by. For a list of possible options, see the Transaction Types section.

EnhancedApi_transactionTypeParam.name: type
EnhancedApi_transactionTypeParam.description: The TransactionType to filter by. For a list of possible options, see the Transaction Types section.

EnhancedApi_limitNewParam.name: limit
EnhancedApi_limitNewParam.description: The number of transactions to retrieve. The value should be between 1 and 100.

Webhooks_limitParam.name: limit
Webhooks_limitParam.description: Maximum number of items to return.

Webhooks_beforeParam.name: before
Webhooks_beforeParam.description: Start searching backwards from this transaction signature.

Webhooks_commitmentParam.name: commitment
Webhooks_commitmentParam.description: How finalized a block must be to be included in the search. If not provided, will default to "finalized" commitment. Note that "processed" level commitment is not supported.

Webhooks_webhookIDParam.name: webhookID
Webhooks_webhookIDParam.description: The webhook ID — a UUIDv4 that uniquely identifies the webhook.

Webhooks_pageNumberParam.name: pageNumber
Webhooks_pageNumberParam.description: The page number used for pagination.

Webhooks_transactionIdParam.name: transaction-id
Webhooks_transactionIdParam.description: The signature of the transaction you wish to query for.

Webhooks_transactionSourceParam.name: source
Webhooks_transactionSourceParam.description: The TransactionSource to filter by. For a list of possible options, see the Transaction Types section.

Webhooks_transactionTypeParam.name: type
Webhooks_transactionTypeParam.description: The TransactionType to filter by. For a list of possible options, see the Transaction Types section.

Webhooks_limitNewParam.name: limit
Webhooks_limitNewParam.description: The number of transactions to retrieve. The value should be between 1 and 100.

WalletApi_WalletOrDomain.name: wallet
WalletApi_WalletOrDomain.description:
Solana wallet address (base58 encoded) or SNS/ANS domain name (e.g. `toly.sol`,
`miester.bonk`). Domain resolution is mainnet-only. Any non-`.sol` domain is treated
as an ANS custom TLD — there is no fixed TLD whitelist.

apiKeyParam.name: api-key
apiKeyParam.description: Helius API key. Obtain from https://dashboard.helius.dev or via `helius signup --json`.

EnhancedApi_404-NotFound.description: The specified resource was not found.

EnhancedApi_500-InternalServerError.description: The server encountered an unexpected condition that prevented it from fulfilling the request.

Webhooks_404-NotFound.description: The specified resource was not found.

Webhooks_500-InternalServerError.description: The server encountered an unexpected condition that prevented it from fulfilling the request.

tags.name: NFTs
tags.description: Access comprehensive NFT data including events, collection aggregations, real-time stats, and complete historical activity on Solana.

tags.name: Tokens
tags.description: Complete token account data, on-chain and off-chain metadata, and detailed information for both fungible and non-fungible Solana tokens.

tags.name: Transactions
tags.description: Enhanced and human-readable transaction histories with decoded instruction data and detailed context.

tags.name: Addresses
tags.description: Enhanced on-chain identity data with complete wallet activity and ownership information.

tags.name: Webhooks
tags.description: Configure real-time blockchain notifications for any Solana address, transaction type, or on-chain event with customizable delivery options.

tags.name: Identity
tags.description: Lookup wallet identities and known addresses
